title: tax — field classification
description: Tax carries FINANCIAL RECORDS + LEGAL DOCUMENTS. tax_amount + rate percentage are competitor intel (revealing tenant tax posture). Certificate numbers + documents are RESTRICTED (legal identifiers). Provider credentials are RESTRICTED (financial-critical). Every calculation is a REGULATED_FINANCIAL record with 7-10 year retention.

confidential: TaxRate.percentage (competitor intel — reveals a tenant's tax posture per jurisdiction; bucketed on egress + never carried in analytics)
TaxJurisdiction.registration_number (the tenant's own VAT/GST/EIN number — reveals tenant's tax jurisdiction posture)
TaxCalculation.source_amount_cents (revenue amount — competitor tenant cross-inference risk)
TaxCalculation.tax_amount_cents (computed tax — same risk as source amount)
TaxCalculation.tax_rate_snapshot (contains the rate percentage + rate_id — same rationale as TaxRate.percentage)
TaxCalculation.exemption_applied (contains certificate_number redacted, but the fact-of-exemption is competitor intel)
TaxCalculation.provider_metadata (may contain provider-side identifiers that map back to the tenant's account)
restricted: TaxExemption.certificate_number (LEGAL IDENTIFIER — cannot be freely distributed; support role sees redacted view)

TaxExemption.certificate_document_url (ENCRYPTED AT REST — encrypted S3 URL pointer. Access via time-limited signed URLs only. NEVER decrypted in logs, notifications, broadcasts, or wire projections.)
regulated_financial: TaxCalculation.source_amount_cents + source_currency (financial audit — 7-year retention; 10 for Enterprise)
TaxCalculation.tax_rate_snapshot (frozen rate for regulator audit — 7-year retention)
TaxCalculation.exemption_applied (frozen exemption for regulator audit — 7-year retention)
TaxCalculation.provider (which provider computed the tax — regulator audit)
TaxRate.percentage + effective_from + effective_to (historical rate audit — indefinite retention while active + 7 years post-expiry)
audit_log: TaxJurisdiction.registration_number (every change — the tenant's own VAT/GST/EIN)
TaxExemption.verification_status (every transition — pending → verified / rejected / expired)
TaxExemption.certificate_number + certificate_document_url (every mutation — encrypted in audit blob)
TaxCalculation.* (every write — but there are no updates on tax_calculations, only creates; hence audit only captures the create)
provider config credentials (every mutation — encrypted in audit blob)

notes: TaxRate.percentage is CONFIDENTIAL not RESTRICTED because it's shared with the customer at invoice display time (e.g. 'VAT 20%'). But raw percentage NEVER flows through analytics or broadcasts — it's bucketed. Reasoning: aggregate percentage exposure across N tenants would reveal each tenant's tax posture to competitors.
TaxExemption.certificate_number is RESTRICTED because certificate numbers are legally identifying documents. A leaked reseller certificate could be reused fraudulently.

TaxExemption.certificate_document_url is RESTRICTED (highest classification) — the certificate PDF itself is a legal document. Encryption at rest is mandatory; the URL field is NEVER included in broadcasts, notifications, or audit blobs (audit blob stores the encrypted ciphertext, not the underlying signed URL).

TaxCalculation.tax_rate_snapshot + exemption_applied are FROZEN JSONB blobs — the entire snapshot is regulated_financial. This is deliberate: regulator audit must see EXACTLY what applied at issuance, not a reconstruction from current-day rates.
PCI-DSS scope: NO card / PAN / CVV fields on any tax row. TaxCalculation.source_amount_cents is a currency amount only. Payment references upstream `transaction` module tokens (Wave 4 peer).
regulated_financial tagging applies to every tax_calculations field. The retention pipeline honours the 7-year rule (10 for Enterprise) on the entire row.
TaxJurisdiction.registration_number (the tenant's own VAT/GST/EIN number) is CONFIDENTIAL — competitor intel that reveals a tenant's tax registration posture.
